content: read reviews/863.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/863.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/863.md.

1ŌåÆ# Review: deepmind/863.lean
     2ŌåÆ
     3ŌåÆ## 1. Copyright Header
     4ŌåÆ
     5ŌåÆ- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     6ŌåÆ- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
     7ŌåÆ
     8ŌåÆ## 2. Import Statement
     9ŌåÆ

    11ŌåÆ- [x] No other imports are present.
    12ŌåÆ
    13ŌåÆ## 3. Module Docstring
    14ŌåÆ
    15ŌåÆ- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
    16ŌåÆ- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼æs Problem 863`.
    17ŌåÆ- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/863`.
    18ŌåÆ- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (BŌéé[r] sets vs difference-bounded sets, limits, and the Berend/Freud connection).
    19ŌåÆ- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er92c]`.
    20ŌåÆ
    21ŌåÆ## 4. Namespace
    22ŌåÆ
    23ŌåÆ- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos863 ... end Erdos863`.
    24ŌåÆ- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos863`).
    25ŌåÆ- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block (lines 31, 33: `open Finset Filter` and `open scoped Topology`).
    26ŌåÆ
    27ŌåÆ## 5. Custom Definitions
    28ŌåÆ
    29ŌåÆ- [x] All helper definitions have LaTeX docstrings (`/-- ... -/`): `sumRepCount`, `IsB2r`, `maxB2rSize`, `diffRepCount`, `IsDiffBounded`, `maxDiffBoundedSize`.
    30ŌåÆ- [x] Definitions involving classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`: `sumRepCount`, `maxB2rSize`, `diffRepCount`, `maxDiffBoundedSize`. (`IsB2r` and `IsDiffBounded` are `Prop`-valued and don't require it.)
    31ŌåÆ- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    32ŌåÆ- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsB2r`, `IsDiffBounded`.
    33ŌåÆ- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`: `sumRepCount`, `maxB2rSize`, `diffRepCount`, `maxDiffBoundedSize`.
    34ŌåÆ- [x] No problem-specific notation needed; N/A.
    35ŌåÆ- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions. (Optional ŌĆö not required.)
    36ŌåÆ
    37ŌåÆ## 6. Theorem Statements
    38ŌåÆ
    39ŌåÆ- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
    40ŌåÆ- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_863` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
    41ŌåÆ- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
    42ŌåÆ- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`.
    43ŌåÆ- [x] Main theorem and variant both have docstrings.
    44ŌåÆ- [x] Theorem docstrings reference source paper: `[Er92c]`.
    45ŌåÆ
    46ŌåÆ### `answer()` usage
    47ŌåÆ
    48ŌåÆThe problem asks whether two limiting constants are different (a yes/no question: "Is it true that c_r ŌēĀ c_r' for r Ōēź 2?"). **Case 1** applies.
    49ŌåÆ
    50ŌåÆ- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) Ōåö P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked.
    51ŌåÆ- [x] `answer(sorry)` is used (problem is open).
    52ŌåÆ- [x] The RHS formalizes the question so that `answer(True)` would mean "yes, c_r ŌēĀ c_r'" and `answer(False)` would mean "no".
    53ŌåÆ- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
    54ŌåÆ
    55ŌåÆ## 7. Category Attribute
    56ŌåÆ
    57ŌåÆ- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
    58ŌåÆ- [x] The value is valid: `research open`.

    64ŌåÆ- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `5 11`.
    65ŌåÆ- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**: 5, 11.
    66ŌåÆ- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    67ŌåÆ- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 11 (number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.
    68ŌåÆ
    69ŌåÆ## 9. Proof Bodies
    70ŌåÆ
    71ŌåÆ- [x] Open conjectures use multi-line `by` / `sorry` form (lines 76ŌĆō77, 91ŌĆō92).
    72ŌåÆ- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    73ŌåÆ- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems.
    74ŌåÆ- [x] No `native_decide`.
    75ŌåÆ
    76ŌåÆ## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    77ŌåÆ
    78ŌåÆ- [x] Variant is named `erdos_863.variants.strong`.
    79ŌåÆ- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
    80ŌåÆ- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]` attributes.
    81ŌåÆ- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it is the strong form (c' < c instead of c ŌēĀ c').
    82ŌåÆ
    83ŌåÆ## 11. Code Quality
    84ŌåÆ
    85ŌåÆ- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    86ŌåÆ- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`ŌłĆ`, `Ōēź`, `ŌäĢ`, `ŌäØ`, `ŌēĀ`, `Ōåö`, `ŌåÆ`).
    87ŌåÆ- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    88ŌåÆ- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
    89ŌåÆ- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
    90ŌåÆ- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    91ŌåÆ
    92ŌåÆ## 12. Build Verification
    93ŌåÆ
    94ŌåÆ- [ ] `lake build` not run in this review. No obvious issues; file structure and syntax are correct.
    95ŌåÆ- [ ] Custom linter conformance not verified via build. All patterns match expected linter requirements by inspection.
    96ŌåÆ
    97ŌåÆ## Summary
    98ŌåÆ

   100ŌåÆ
   101ŌåÆThe file is clean and fully conformant with the checklist. All required items pass. The only unchecked boxes are the optional `@[category test/API]` sanity checks on definitions and the build verification (not run). No issues found.

# Review: deepmind/863.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼æs Problem 863`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/863`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (BŌéé[r] sets vs difference-bounded sets, limits, and the Berend/Freud connection).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er92c]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos863 ... end Erdos863`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos863`).
- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block (lines 31, 33: `open Finset Filter` and `open scoped Topology`).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] All helper definitions have LaTeX docstrings (`/-- ... -/`): `sumRepCount`, `IsB2r`, `maxB2rSize`, `diffRepCount`, `IsDiffBounded`, `maxDiffBoundedSize`.
- [x] Definitions involving classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`: `sumRepCount`, `maxB2rSize`, `diffRepCount`, `maxDiffBoundedSize`. (`IsB2r` and `IsDiffBounded` are `Prop`-valued and don't require it.)
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsB2r`, `IsDiffBounded`.
- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`: `sumRepCount`, `maxB2rSize`, `diffRepCount`, `maxDiffBoundedSize`.
- [x] No problem-specific notation needed; N/A.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions. (Optional ŌĆö not required.)

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_863` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem and variant both have docstrings.
- [x] Theorem docstrings reference source paper: `[Er92c]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks whether two limiting constants are different (a yes/no question: "Is it true that c_r ŌēĀ c_r' for r Ōēź 2?"). **Case 1** applies.

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) Ōåö P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked.
- [x] `answer(sorry)` is used (problem is open).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question so that `answer(True)` would mean "yes, c_r ŌēĀ c_r'" and `answer(False)` would mean "no".
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] The value is valid: `research open`.
- [x] `research open` theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `5 11`.
- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**: 5, 11.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 11 (number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjectures use multi-line `by` / `sorry` form (lines 76ŌĆō77, 91ŌĆō92).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant is named `erdos_863.variants.strong`.
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it is the strong form (c' < c instead of c ŌēĀ c').

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`ŌłĆ`, `Ōēź`, `ŌäĢ`, `ŌäØ`, `ŌēĀ`, `Ōåö`, `ŌåÆ`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run in this review. No obvious issues; file structure and syntax are correct.
- [ ] Custom linter conformance not verified via build. All patterns match expected linter requirements by inspection.

## Summary

**Result: PASS**

The file is clean and fully conformant with the checklist. All required items pass. The only unchecked boxes are the optional `@[category test/API]` sanity checks on definitions and the build verification (not run). No issues found.

    15ŌåÆ-/
    16ŌåÆ
    17ŌåÆimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18ŌåÆ
    19ŌåÆ/-!
    20ŌåÆ# Erd┼æs Problem 863
    21ŌåÆ
    22ŌåÆ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/863](https://www.erdosproblems.com/863)
    23ŌåÆ
    24ŌåÆA problem of Erd┼æs [Er92c], first formulated in conversation with Berend, and later
    25ŌåÆindependently reformulated with Freud. It is known that $c_1 = c_1' = 1$ (the classical
    26ŌåÆbound on Sidon sets).
    27ŌåÆ
    28ŌåÆ[Er92c] Erd┼æs, P.
    29ŌåÆ-/
    30ŌåÆ
    31ŌåÆopen Finset Filter
    32ŌåÆ
    33ŌåÆopen scoped Topology
    34ŌåÆ
    35ŌåÆnamespace Erdos863
    36ŌåÆ
    37ŌåÆ/-- The number of representations of $n$ as $a + b$ with $a \leq b$ and $a, b \in S$. -/
    38ŌåÆnoncomputable def sumRepCount (S : Finset ŌäĢ) (n : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
    39ŌåÆ  S.sum (fun a => (S.filter (fun b => a Ōēż b Ōł¦ a + b = n)).card)
    40ŌåÆ
    41ŌåÆ/-- $S$ is a $B_2[r]$ set if every $n$ has at most $r$ representations
    42ŌåÆ    as $a + b$ with $a \leq b$, $a, b \in S$. -/
    43ŌåÆdef IsB2r (r : ŌäĢ) (S : Finset ŌäĢ) : Prop :=
    44ŌåÆ  ŌłĆ n : ŌäĢ, sumRepCount S n Ōēż r
    45ŌåÆ
    46ŌåÆ/-- The maximum cardinality of a $B_2[r]$ subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$. -/
    47ŌåÆnoncomputable def maxB2rSize (r : ŌäĢ) (N : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
    48ŌåÆ  ((Icc 1 N).powerset.filter (fun S => IsB2r r S)).sup Finset.card
    49ŌåÆ
    50ŌåÆ/-- The number of representations of a positive integer $n$ as a difference $a - b = n$
    51ŌåÆ    (equivalently, $a = b + n$) with $a, b \in S$. -/
    52ŌåÆnoncomputable def diffRepCount (S : Finset ŌäĢ) (n : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
    53ŌåÆ  S.sum (fun b => (S.filter (fun a => a = b + n)).card)
    54ŌåÆ
    55ŌåÆ/-- $S$ is difference-$r$-bounded if every nonzero difference has at most $r$
    56ŌåÆ    representations as $a - b$ with $a, b \in S$. -/
    57ŌåÆdef IsDiffBounded (r : ŌäĢ) (S : Finset ŌäĢ) : Prop :=
    58ŌåÆ  ŌłĆ n : ŌäĢ, n > 0 ŌåÆ diffRepCount S n Ōēż r
    59ŌåÆ
    60ŌåÆ/-- The maximum cardinality of a difference-$r$-bounded subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$. -/
    61ŌåÆnoncomputable def maxDiffBoundedSize (r : ŌäĢ) (N : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
    62ŌåÆ  ((Icc 1 N).powerset.filter (fun S => IsDiffBounded r S)).sup Finset.card
    63ŌåÆ
    64ŌåÆ/--
    65ŌåÆErd┼æs Problem 863, weak form [Er92c]:
    66ŌåÆ
    67ŌåÆIf the limits $c_r = \lim_{N \to \infty} f_r^+(N) / \sqrt{N}$ (maximum $B_2[r]$ set size)
    68ŌåÆand $c_r' = \lim_{N \to \infty} f_r^-(N) / \sqrt{N}$ (maximum difference-$r$-bounded set
    69ŌåÆsize) both exist, then $c_r \neq c_r'$ for $r \geq 2$.
    70ŌåÆ-/
    71ŌåÆ@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    72ŌåÆtheorem erdos_863 : answer(sorry) Ōåö
    73ŌåÆ    ŌłĆ (r : ŌäĢ), r Ōēź 2 ŌåÆ ŌłĆ (c c' : ŌäØ),
    74ŌåÆ    Tendsto (fun N => (maxB2rSize r N : ŌäØ) / Real.sqrt (N : ŌäØ)) atTop (nhds c) ŌåÆ
    75ŌåÆ    Tendsto (fun N => (maxDiffBoundedSize r N : ŌäØ) / Real.sqrt (N : ŌäØ)) atTop (nhds c') ŌåÆ
    76ŌåÆ    c ŌēĀ c' := by
    77ŌåÆ  sorry
    78ŌåÆ
    79ŌåÆ/--
    80ŌåÆErd┼æs Problem 863, strong form [Er92c]:
    81ŌåÆ
    82ŌåÆIf the limits $c_r = \lim_{N \to \infty} f_r^+(N) / \sqrt{N}$ (maximum $B_2[r]$ set size)
    83ŌåÆand $c_r' = \lim_{N \to \infty} f_r^-(N) / \sqrt{N}$ (maximum difference-$r$-bounded set
    84ŌåÆsize) both exist, then $c_r' < c_r$ for $r \geq 2$.
    85ŌåÆ-/
    86ŌåÆ@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    87ŌåÆtheorem erdos_863.variants.strong : answer(sorry) Ōåö
    88ŌåÆ    ŌłĆ (r : ŌäĢ), r Ōēź 2 ŌåÆ ŌłĆ (c c' : ŌäØ),
    89ŌåÆ    Tendsto (fun N => (maxB2rSize r N : ŌäØ) / Real.sqrt (N : ŌäØ)) atTop (nhds c) ŌåÆ
    90ŌåÆ    Tendsto (fun N => (maxDiffBoundedSize r N : ŌäØ) / Real.sqrt (N : ŌäØ)) atTop (nhds c') ŌåÆ
    91ŌåÆ    c' < c := by
    92ŌåÆ  sorry
    93ŌåÆ
    94ŌåÆend Erdos863
    95ŌåÆ

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd┼æs Problem 863

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/863](https://www.erdosproblems.com/863)

A problem of Erd┼æs [Er92c], first formulated in conversation with Berend, and later
independently reformulated with Freud. It is known that $c_1 = c_1' = 1$ (the classical
bound on Sidon sets).

[Er92c] Erd┼æs, P.
-/

open Finset Filter

open scoped Topology

namespace Erdos863

/-- The number of representations of $n$ as $a + b$ with $a \leq b$ and $a, b \in S$. -/
noncomputable def sumRepCount (S : Finset ŌäĢ) (n : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
  S.sum (fun a => (S.filter (fun b => a Ōēż b Ōł¦ a + b = n)).card)

/-- $S$ is a $B_2[r]$ set if every $n$ has at most $r$ representations
    as $a + b$ with $a \leq b$, $a, b \in S$. -/
def IsB2r (r : ŌäĢ) (S : Finset ŌäĢ) : Prop :=
  ŌłĆ n : ŌäĢ, sumRepCount S n Ōēż r

/-- The maximum cardinality of a $B_2[r]$ subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$. -/
noncomputable def maxB2rSize (r : ŌäĢ) (N : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
  ((Icc 1 N).powerset.filter (fun S => IsB2r r S)).sup Finset.card

/-- The number of representations of a positive integer $n$ as a difference $a - b = n$
    (equivalently, $a = b + n$) with $a, b \in S$. -/
noncomputable def diffRepCount (S : Finset ŌäĢ) (n : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
  S.sum (fun b => (S.filter (fun a => a = b + n)).card)

/-- $S$ is difference-$r$-bounded if every nonzero difference has at most $r$
    representations as $a - b$ with $a, b \in S$. -/
def IsDiffBounded (r : ŌäĢ) (S : Finset ŌäĢ) : Prop :=
  ŌłĆ n : ŌäĢ, n > 0 ŌåÆ diffRepCount S n Ōēż r

/-- The maximum cardinality of a difference-$r$-bounded subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$. -/
noncomputable def maxDiffBoundedSize (r : ŌäĢ) (N : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
  ((Icc 1 N).powerset.filter (fun S => IsDiffBounded r S)).sup Finset.card

/--
Erd┼æs Problem 863, weak form [Er92c]:

If the limits $c_r = \lim_{N \to \infty} f_r^+(N) / \sqrt{N}$ (maximum $B_2[r]$ set size)
and $c_r' = \lim_{N \to \infty} f_r^-(N) / \sqrt{N}$ (maximum difference-$r$-bounded set
size) both exist, then $c_r \neq c_r'$ for $r \geq 2$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
theorem erdos_863 : answer(sorry) Ōåö
    ŌłĆ (r : ŌäĢ), r Ōēź 2 ŌåÆ ŌłĆ (c c' : ŌäØ),
    Tendsto (fun N => (maxB2rSize r N : ŌäØ) / Real.sqrt (N : ŌäØ)) atTop (nhds c) ŌåÆ
    Tendsto (fun N => (maxDiffBoundedSize r N : ŌäØ) / Real.sqrt (N : ŌäØ)) atTop (nhds c') ŌåÆ
    c ŌēĀ c' := by
  sorry

/--
Erd┼æs Problem 863, strong form [Er92c]:

If the limits $c_r = \lim_{N \to \infty} f_r^+(N) / \sqrt{N}$ (maximum $B_2[r]$ set size)
and $c_r' = \lim_{N \to \infty} f_r^-(N) / \sqrt{N}$ (maximum difference-$r$-bounded set
size) both exist, then $c_r' < c_r$ for $r \geq 2$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
theorem erdos_863.variants.strong : answer(sorry) Ōåö
    ŌłĆ (r : ŌäĢ), r Ōēź 2 ŌåÆ ŌłĆ (c c' : ŌäØ),
    Tendsto (fun N => (maxB2rSize r N : ŌäØ) / Real.sqrt (N : ŌäØ)) atTop (nhds c) ŌåÆ
    Tendsto (fun N => (maxDiffBoundedSize r N : ŌäØ) / Real.sqrt (N : ŌäØ)) atTop (nhds c') ŌåÆ
    c' < c := by
  sorry

end Erdos863

content.text: The review result is **PASS**. No changes needed.
